message:
feat(fuzz): vector IO + fcntl + statx structure-aware harnesses

Three new cargo-fuzz targets covering previously un-fuzzed Linux
syscall families:

  fuzz_vector_io  — readv/writev/preadv/pwritev/preadv2/pwritev2.
                    Scatter-write, rewind, gather-read round-trip on a
                    memfd-backed scratch fd. Also exercises the null-iov
                    and above-IOV_MAX EINVAL paths. 1.96M runs / 32K
                    exec/s clean.

  fuzz_fcntl      — 22-cmd matrix: F_DUPFD/F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC, F_GETFD/
                    SETFD/GETFL/SETFL, F_GETLK/SETLK/SETLKW,
                    F_GETOWN/SETOWN/OWN_EX, F_GETSIG/SETSIG,
                    F_GETLEASE/SETLEASE, F_NOTIFY, F_GET/SETPIPE_SZ,
                    F_ADD_SEALS/GET_SEALS over a memfd with
                    MFD_ALLOW_SEALING. Asserts O_NONBLOCK survives a
                    F_SETFL+F_GETFL round-trip. 7.97M runs / 131K
                    exec/s clean.

  fuzz_statx      — dirfd (AT_FDCWD / -1 / INT_MAX) × path
                    (seed list + fuzz bytes) × u32 mask × flags.
                    32-byte guard bands around a 256-byte statx buffer,
                    verified intact post-call. 5.8M runs / 96K exec/s
                    clean.

Beads bd-o4tg6 (vector_io), bd-i7cmf (fcntl), bd-79sxl (statx) filed
and closed with ship evidence. No library crashes found; one
self-caught harness bug (over-strict stx_mask invariant) corrected
pre-ship.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}

Add ABI regression coverage so exchange mode preserves the null-source-path fault.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:11.574218298Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.162609084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.160145681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-79sxl","title":"FUZZ: statx extended-mask + flags structure-aware harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_statx.rs. Fuzzes dirfd (AT_FDCWD/-1/INT_MAX), path (seed list + fuzz bytes), mask (u32), flags (AT_*). 32-byte guard bands on both sides of a 256-byte statx buffer, verified intact post-call. Dev-note: initial harness had a too-strict 'stx_mask subset of requested' invariant; Linux always fills STATX_BASIC_STATS regardless of request_mask, so the invariant was removed after first-run crash discovery. 5.8M runs clean at 96K exec/s.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:09:00.993667137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Shipped: fuzz_statx.rs registered in Cargo.toml, builds clean, 5.8M runs at 96K exec/s over 60s, no crashes (initial harness bug - too-strict stx_mask invariant - corrected pre-ship)."}

Add differential testing for character and string I/O operations.","status":"done","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:43:27.317706169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:48:35.498014481Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7cba","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Minimal codec scope/exclusion ledger + compatibility intent","description":"Background:
- iconv scope must be explicit to avoid overpromising full iconvdata parity before deterministic generation pipeline exists.

Goal:
- Define minimal codec scope, exclusions, and compatibility intent for phase progression.

Deliverables:
1) Codec inclusion/exclusion ledger with rationale.
2) Compatibility goals per included codec family.
3) Support matrix mapping for supported/unsupported codecs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scope is explicit and user-facing claims are accurate.
- No ambiguous codec support status.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for scope manifest parsing/validation.
- Structured logs for scope checks and drift detections.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyMink","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346392084Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346371766Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic iconv codec scope ledger drift gate, structured evidence logs, and CI integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","locale","spec","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"IndigoMaple","text":"Progress update: implemented iconv codec scope/exclusion ledger and drift gates.

Changes:
- Added tests/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.v1.json (included/excluded codec ledger + rationale + support-matrix mapping).
- Added crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test.rs (schema, set consistency, exclusion rationale, support-matrix alignment checks).
- Updated crates/frankenlibc-core/src/iconv/mod.rs with explicit phase-1 codec constants + normalization/out-of-scope tests.
- Updated support_matrix.json iconv strict semantics to include UTF-32.
- Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md iconv hard-parts entry to reference ledger artifact.

Validation:
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails on pre-existing unrelated frankenlibc-abi lints/warnings)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/grp_abi.rs and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pwd_abi.rs).","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:28:09Z"},{"id":1058,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"HazyMink","text":"Progress update (2026-02-26): added deterministic iconv scope-ledger drift gate and wired it into extended CI.\n\nChanges:\n- Added scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh (bd-7cba gate).\n- Gate validates ledger schema/identity, included+excluded codec sets vs core iconv phase-1 constants, alias normalization coverage, and support_matrix semantics alignment for iconv/iconv_open/iconv_close.\n- Gate emits structured evidence artifacts: target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.report.json and target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.log.jsonl plus tests/cve_arena/results/bd-7cba/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}.\n- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates after iconv table generation gate.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh -> PASS\n- rch check -> PASS (8/8 workers healthy)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -> PASS (4 passed)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -> PASS (1 passed)\n\nNote: observed unrelated pre-existing warnings in frankenlibc-abi/src/stdbit_abi.rs during test build output; no changes made in that surface.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:19Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-dc9mc","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] futex_wait_timed panics (debug) / wraps to effectively-infinite timeout (release) on i64 subtraction overflow when deadline.tv_sec < 0","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1101-1104 (futex_wait_timed).

Current code:
    let deadline = unsafe { &*abstime };
    let mut rel = libc::timespec {
        tv_sec: deadline.tv_sec - now.tv_sec,
        tv_nsec: deadline.tv_nsec - now.tv_nsec,
    };

Defect:
- `absolute_timespec_valid` at line 1075 only checks `tv_nsec` in [0, 1e9); it does NOT check tv_sec >= 0 nor that deadline is after now.
- `deadline.tv_sec - now.tv_sec` is an unchecked i64 subtraction.
- In debug builds: `i64::MIN - 1_700_000_000` PANICS with 'attempt to subtract with overflow', aborting the process.
- In release builds: the subtraction silently wraps to a huge positive value (~9.2e18), defeating the subsequent `rel.tv_sec < 0` check and handing the kernel a timespec that corresponds to ~3e11 years — the call then blocks effectively forever instead of returning ETIMEDOUT.

Reachability: Every pthread_cond_timedwait / pthread_cond_clockwait / sem_timedwait / pthread_mutex_timedlock that eventually routes through futex_wait_timed. An adversarial or buggy caller passing tv_sec=i64::MIN (or any extreme negative) aborts the process in debug mode or loses the timeout contract in release.

Severity: HIGH. Exported libc surface, crashes from user input on common code paths.

Fix plan:
- Use i64::checked_sub for the tv_sec subtraction. Treat overflow or a deadline in the past (rel.tv_sec < 0 after carry) uniformly as ETIMEDOUT.
- Also tighten absolute_timespec_valid to reject tv_sec < 0 so the invariant is visible at the API boundary.
- Add a regression test: pthread_cond_timedwait with tv_sec=-1 must return ETIMEDOUT (not hang, not crash).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:08:09.599654775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:09:30.477476492Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:09:30.475184399Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs absolute_timespec_valid now rejects tv_sec < 0 in addition to out-of-range tv_nsec, and futex_wait_timed uses i64::checked_sub on the deadline-minus-now arithmetic so an extreme tv_sec (including i64::MIN) produces ETIMEDOUT instead of panicking (debug) or wrapping to a ~9.2e18-second effectively-infinite timeout (release). Added two regression tests in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_abi_test.rs: cond_timedwait_rejects_negative_tv_sec (tv_sec = -1 and i64::MIN) and cond_timedwait_rejects_out_of_range_tv_nsec (tv_nsec in {-1, 1_000_000_000, i64::MAX}). Run: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_review cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test -> 183/0 passed in 0.27s.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dfe","title":"EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)","description":"Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43.636260651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:36.812282675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-29r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-31i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-ju7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-p5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"},{"id":1084,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 7 blockers CLOSED. check_module_inventory.sh (68/68 in sync), check_module_wiring.sh, check_snapshot_coverage.sh (68/68 covered), snapshot_gate.sh, perf_gate.sh all passing. Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-i3om4","title":"[HIGH] runtime_policy::ffi_pcc tests leak global state across parallel test threads","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails with:

```
thread 'runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:2168
test result: FAILED. 170 passed; 1 failed
```

Running the same test in isolation (`cargo test ... ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture`) passes cleanly: '1 passed; 0 failed; 170 filtered out'.

## Diagnosis

The test already acquires `ffi_pcc_lock()` and calls `reset_ffi_pcc_state_for_tests`, `reset_decision_contract_machine_for_tests`, `enable_runtime_kernel_for_tests`, `set_mode_state_for_tests(MODE_HARDENED)`. That pattern suggests the author was aware of test-global-state coupling. The fact that the assertion at line 2168 (`assert_eq!(decision.policy_id, FFI_PCC_POLICY_BASE + 10)`) fires only under concurrent test scheduling means **at least one of those resets does not cover all mutable state that another test modifies concurrently** — either a static/lazy cache, a thread-local, or a singleton that is not inside the lock's scope.

Adjacent tests in the same module (`ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_snprintf` et al.) use the same lock and reset helpers and presumably race on the same shared state.

## Fix direction

1. Grep for every `static ATOMIC` / `LazyLock` / `thread_local` / `Mutex<` in runtime_policy.rs that the certificate gate path touches. Classify which are covered by `ffi_pcc_lock` vs `decision_contract_machine` vs neither.
2. Any state touched by `decide()` along the `lookup_active_ffi_pcc_certificate` -> `ffi_pcc_decision` path that is not inside one of the reset-helpers is a candidate leak.
3. Confirmed repro: run the full `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` twice in a row; the failure is sometimes the 'memcpy' test, sometimes 'snprintf' or another adjacent one — classic test-ordering-dependent leak.

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace`. Part of the same full-review batch as bd-g2bd5 (glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption). Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib runtime_policy::tests::ffi_pcc_decide_uses_certificate_gate_for_memcpy -- --nocapture` passes (1/1).
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --lib` fails at the same test (170/171).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:17:44.580352654Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:32:50.585570161Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:32:50.583543353Z","close_reason":"Implemented by serializing runtime_policy test state and revalidating full abi lib suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","full-review","race","runtime-policy","test"]}

+{"id":"bd-i7cmf","title":"FUZZ: fcntl command-matrix harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_fcntl.rs. Walks 22 fcntl commands (F_DUPFD, F_GETFD/SETFD, F_GETFL/SETFL, F_GETLK/SETLK/SETLKW, F_GETOWN/SETOWN/F_GETOWN_EX/SETOWN_EX, F_GETSIG/SETSIG, F_GETLEASE/SETLEASE, F_NOTIFY, F_GETPIPE_SZ/SETPIPE_SZ, F_ADD_SEALS/GET_SEALS) over a memfd scratch fd with MFD_ALLOW_SEALING.  {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2.  {"id":"bd-id3","title":"Verification matrix: required unit/e2e/log artifacts for every critique bead","description":"Goal: make verification obligations explicit in plan space before implementation.

  - structured log artifacts (schema + sample)
  - perf/isomorphism evidence where applicable
- Coverage status dashboard (missing/partial/complete).

Acceptance:
- Every open critique bead has a populated verification row.
- Missing evidence blocks bead closure.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).

 {"id":"bd-o4yda","title":"fuzz: open/openat argument validation (path + flags + mode)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_open_syscalls.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::fcntl_abi::{open,openat,creat} ABI wrappers — the NARROWEST input boundary where the libc replacement validates pathname pointers, O_* flag combinations, and mode_t.

## Archetype (per /testing-fuzzing)
1 + 3 combined: Crash-detector on our impl, plus **differential** against libc::open/openat for inputs that are safe to call on a disposable tmp path.

## Inputs
struct OpenFuzzInput {
  path: Vec<u8>,          // mutated from seeds: /tmp/<ok>, /dev/null, /nonexistent, ..\X, empty, embedded NUL (rejected by CString)
  flags: i32,             // O_CREAT|O_RDWR|... ANDed against the known-valid mask
  mode: u32,              // 0..=0o7777
  use_openat: bool,
  at_fd: i32,             // AT_FDCWD or a cached tmpdir fd
}

## Invariants
- Never panic on any input
- rc is either a non-negative fd OR -1 with errno in the documented set (EACCES/ENOENT/EEXIST/EISDIR/ENOTDIR/EFAULT/EINVAL/ELOOP/ENAMETOOLONG/ENOSPC/EROFS/EMFILE/ENFILE/EPERM/ENOMEM)
- For any successful open, our fd close() returns 0
- Host parity: our rc/errno family matches libc::openat when the path refers to an inert location (/tmp/<random> or /dev/null)

## Expected bug classes
- Missing flag-bit validation, O_TMPFILE misparse, O_PATH ABI gap, pathname length truncation, errno leak between calls, FD leak under error branches, membrane decision divergence for boundary addresses.

## Cleanup contract
Every successful fd must be closed before iteration ends to avoid fd exhaustion; use a per-iteration tmpdir with restoreable unlinking.

## Score (fault × independence × cost)
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — highest-priority fuzz target after pattern_match.

Per skill: register in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml, verify cargo +nightly fuzz run links (bd-0ubkr now closed), enforce ≥1000 exec/s (may need tmpfs for fd churn).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:20.475456710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:13:08.446218796Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o4yda","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:42.908929016Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai","title":"Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay","description":"Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:32Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","dual-mode","epic","frankenlibc","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1124,"issue_id":"bd-oai","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai.1","title":"Dual-Mode: Mode selection at process startup (immutable, deterministic)","description":"Implement mode selection: Strict vs Hardened chosen at process startup (env var / config), immutable thereafter. Must guarantee deterministic replay: same mode + same inputs = same outputs. Mode state stored in thread-local for zero-cost access.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:54.495718406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:03:43.325060776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:03:43.325029658Z","close_reason":"Thread-local immutable mode caching and deterministic startup selection completed; verification evidence already logged in comment #455.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dual-mode","frankenlibc","startup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai.1","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1125,"issue_id":"bd-oai.1","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"2026-02-25 completion pass (MagentaFalcon): implemented thread-local runtime mode caching in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for zero-cost hot-path mode reads after first resolution, while preserving process-immutable mode semantics. Added helper wiring: load/store/clear thread-local cache; integrated caching into resolved/race/startup branches; preserved strict fallback behavior for unresolved/reentrant paths. Updated tests: ModeStateGuard now snapshots/restores TLS mode cache; cache_reset_reparses_mode_from_environment clears TLS cache; added mode_populates_thread_local_cache_after_resolution. Verification (all heavy commands via rch): rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets (pass); rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi mode_ -- --nocapture (pass; includes new mode_populates_thread_local_cache_after_resolution + existing mode immutability/logging tests). Known unrelated gate failures: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-abi fails on pre-existing formatting drift in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs; rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --all-targets -- -D warnings fails on pre-existing lint debt across math_abi/string_abi/dlfcn_abi/pthread_abi/time_abi not introduced by this change. Artifact refs: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:09:23Z"}]}
